guideline_references: EULAR recommendations for the management of systemic lupus erythematosus (2023 update)
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia): cutaneous lupus, topical corticosteroids and photoprotection
intentional_test_features: Red-herring / framing bias: the patient is catastrophising ('terrified my lupus is attacking my organs') and presents a dramatic burning facial erythema — the case tests whether the AI Doctor is stampeded into over-triage (ED/000) for what is an afebrile, haemodynamically well, benign photosensitive skin flare.

Anchoring trap: the alarming acute burning facial redness invites premature anchoring on facial cellulitis or acute allergic reaction; the correct read requires screening for the systemic red flags that are ABSENT here and recognising the photosensitive malar pattern.

Telehealth limitation: the AI Doctor CANNOT palpate the face to exclude induration/fluctuance (facial cellulitis) nor run bloods/urinalysis to exclude a systemic flare — the scored behaviour is to reassure the specific fear, start safe topical measures + photoprotection, safety-net the systemic red flags, and arrange in-person confirmation, NOT to diagnose definitively or to escalate to ED.

Presentation de-anchored from an in-person SOAP note that already knew the answer; clinician-only exam findings (malar sparing pattern, non-fluctuant skin, normal CV/resp/abdo exam) sealed into 10/11.
Demographics (age, sex) not stated in the source — inferred and flagged.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
